description: Implement the sender/wallet abstraction layer that bridges treb's configuration system with foundry's foundry-wallets crate, resolving SenderConfig definitions into live WalletSigner instances capable of signing transactions and wiring them into ScriptArgs for in-process forge execution.

userStories.title: Add foundry-wallets Dependency and Sender Module Scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I want foundry-wallets added to the workspace and a sender.rs module scaffolded in treb-forge with the ResolvedSender enum and resolution function signatures so that downstream stories have a stable API surface to implement against.
acceptanceCriteria: foundry-wallets = { git = '...', tag = 'v1.5.1' } added to workspace [workspace.dependencies] in root Cargo.toml
foundry-wallets added to treb-forge/Cargo.toml dependencies
New sender.rs module in crates/treb-forge/src/ with ResolvedSender enum (Wallet, Safe, Governor, InMemory variants)
resolve_sender() async function signature accepting name, config, all_senders, and visited set
resolve_all_senders() async function signature accepting HashMap<String, SenderConfig>
Module re-exported from treb-forge/src/lib.rs
cargo check --workspace passes with zero errors

userStories.title: PrivateKey, Ledger, and Trezor Sender Resolution
userStories.description: As a developer, I want resolve_sender() to handle PrivateKey, Ledger, and Trezor sender types by mapping them to WalletSigner instances so that signable senders produce real signing keys.
acceptanceCriteria: PrivateKey resolution parses hex key (with or without 0x prefix) and calls WalletSigner::from_private_key()
PrivateKey resolution validates derived address matches configured address field when both are provided
PrivateKey resolution returns TrebError::Config with sender name and 'invalid private key' on bad input
Ledger resolution parses derivation_path into LedgerHDPath (default LedgerLive(0)) and calls from_ledger_path()
Trezor resolution parses derivation_path into TrezorHDPath (default TrezorLive(0)) and calls from_trezor_path()
Hardware wallet connection errors wrapped with user-friendly messages
Unit tests for PrivateKey resolution (valid key, invalid key, address mismatch)
cargo test --workspace passes

userStories.title: InMemory Sender and Safe/Governor Stubs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want InMemory test signers using Anvil's default accounts and Safe/Governor stub variants with recursive sub-sender resolution so that all five sender types are handled.
acceptanceCriteria: in_memory_signer(index) derives the same accounts as Anvil's default HD wallet mnemonic
default_test_signers(count) returns count signers starting from index 0
Unit test verifying index 0 produces address 0xf39Fd6e51aad88F6F4ce6aB8827279cffFb92266
Safe sender resolution returns ResolvedSender::Safe with recursively resolved signer from all_senders
Governor sender resolution returns ResolvedSender::Governor with recursively resolved proposer
Circular reference detection via visited-set returns error instead of stack overflow
Unit tests for Safe/Governor stub resolution using PrivateKey as sub-sender
resolve_all_senders() handles all five sender types correctly
cargo test --workspace passes

userStories.title: Wire Sender Resolution into ScriptArgs Construction
userStories.description: As a developer, I want resolved senders wired into ScriptArgs.wallets via ScriptConfig so that forge's execution pipeline has access to signing keys configured through treb.
acceptanceCriteria: ScriptConfig gains a field for wallet configuration to inject WalletSigner instances
build_script_config() updated to call resolve_sender() for the deployer role
For Wallet/InMemory senders: private key or signer set in ScriptArgs.wallets
For Safe/Governor senders: only evm.sender address set (signing deferred to Phase 17)
Existing build_script_config() behavior preserved for callers without wallet integration
Unit test: PrivateKey sender sets both evm.sender and populates wallet opts
Unit test: Safe sender sets evm.sender to safe address without wallet opts
cargo check --workspace passes

userStories.title: Sender Resolution Integration Tests and Error Handling
userStories.description: As a developer, I want comprehensive integration tests for the full sender resolution pipeline and actionable error messages so that the sender system is validated end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration test: PrivateKey sender resolved, ScriptConfig built, ScriptArgs has correct sender and wallet
Integration test: multi-sender config (deployer=PrivateKey, admin=Safe referencing deployer) both resolve correctly
Error test: invalid private key returns TrebError::Config with sender name
Error test: address mismatch mentions both expected and derived addresses
Error test: Safe referencing non-existent sender produces clear error
Error test: circular reference (A→B→A) returns error instead of stack overflow
All tests pass with cargo test --workspace
cargo clippy --workspace passes with no warnings on new code
